LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD GROUP, INC, MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO, INC, PERSHING LLC, D. MERRIMAN, GREGORY CURHAN, AND, ROB FORD MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED. AGREEMENT BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND PERSHING LACKS CHARACTERISTICS OF AGREEMENT IN MACAULEY VS. NORLANDER (1992) 12 CAL.APP. 4TH 1. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON; COURT REPORTER: JOYCE LONG, CSR #8620

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD GROUP, INC., MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO., INC, D.JONATHAN MERRIMAN, GREGORY CURHAN, AND, ROB FORD DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION REGARDING CURHAN AND FORD FOR PLAINTIFF TO SPECIFICALLY PLEAD ALLEGATIONS AGAINST THEM. AS TO THE OTHER DEFENDANTS, SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 1ST, 2ND, 3RD, 4TH, AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION. FRAUD MUST BE PLED WITH SPECIFICITY AND RESULTING DAMAGES MUST BE ADEQUATELY PLED. AS TO 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, ALLEGE SPECIFIC PROVISIONS OF THE CORPORATIONS CODE THAT HAVE BEEN VIOLATED. OVERRULED AS TO 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION (UNJUST ENRICHMENT.) (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD GROUP, INC., MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO., INC, D.JONATHAN MERRIMAN, GREGORY CURHAN, and, ROB FORD DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to all causes of action regarding Curhan and Ford for plaintiff to specifically plead allegations against them. As to the other defendants, sustained with leave to amend as to 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, and 5th causes of action. Fraud must be pled with specificity and resulting damages must be adequately pled. As to 3rd cause of action, allege specific provisions of the Corporations Code that have been violated. Overruled as to 6th cause of action (unjust enrichment.) (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration;
Off Calendar Apr-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD GROUP, INC., MERRIMAN CURHAN FORD & CO., INC, PERSHING LLC, D. MERRIMAN, GREGORY CURHAN, and, ROB FORD Motion To Compel Arbitration. Appearances: John P. Kristensen, Esq. Attorney at Law, (650) 358-5901 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Ramiz I. Rafeedie, Esq. of K&L Gates (310) 552-5000, appearing for defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to compel arbitration is denied. Agreement between Plaintiff and Pershing lacks characteristics of agreement in Macauley vs. Norlander (1992) 12 Cal.App. 4th 1. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/AJR)Judge: A. James Robertson; Court Reporter: Joyce Long, CSR # 8620
